mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and reduces corporate profitability through higher borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, which equity investors interpret as a forward-looking negative signal for corporate earnings and cash flow generation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns following PAYEMS declines in more than 60% of instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating reduced aggregate demand and lower corporate revenue growth. This interaction effect produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST declines month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 709590.0 million and HOUST at 1521.0 thousand as of 2024-03-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-declining instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in a multivariate regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in industrial production below the 102.0 index level signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, constraining corporate profit margins and indicating economic slowdown. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 101.5 and remains below that level for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 101.5, or rolling 3-year Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This threshold crossing triggers equity market repricing downward as investors anticipate lower consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% from a level of 3.8% or below
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following UNRATE > 4.0% crossings, or the mean return differential is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI-U accelerating above 3.5% year-over-year signals persistent price pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase restrictive policy rates, constraining equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real cash flows. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: high-inflation regime with monetary policy constraint
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of high-inflation instances, or the correlation between CPI momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 expanding at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22679.255 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of high-growth instances, or the correlation between GDP growth and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals strengthening consumer demand and construction investment, indicating robust aggregate demand. This interaction effect produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series expand simultaneously, reflecting broad-based economic strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month AND HOUST increases month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 709590.0 million and HOUST at 1521.0 thousand as of 2024-03-15
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-expanding instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in a multivariate regression model.
